title: Republican California Sheriff Seizes Ballots In Election Probe
summary:
Republican California Sheriff Seizes Ballots In Election Probe

            Authored by Evgenia Filimianova via The Epoch Times (emphasis ours),

Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco, who is running to be the next California governor, has seized more than half a million ballots from a November 2025 special election on redistricting, triggering a political and legal confrontation with state officials.

title: US PMIs Signal Stagflation Fears Accelerating As War Started
summary:
US PMIs Signal Stagflation Fears Accelerating As War Started

            With 'hard' US macro data having drifted weaker all year, consensus was expecting only a modest decline in S&P Global's US Composite index in preliminary March data (that presumably will be affected in some part by the war and its consequences).

The consensus was right, but the picture was mixed with Manufacturing PMI surprising to the upside (52.4 vs 51.5 exp vs 51.6 prior) - highest since Oct 2025.

title: Art Of The Dream
summary:
Art Of The Dream

            By Bas van Geffen, Senior Macro Strategist at Rabobank

Trump’s 48-hour deadline turned into a weeklong one. Yesterday, the US president announced that he has “instructed the department of War to postpone any and all military strikes against Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure for a five day period.”

President Trump says he delayed the actions following “very good and productive conversations” with Iran, which he expects to continue throughout the week.

title: Senators Close To Deal To End Airport TSA Crisis As Americans' Fury Mounts
summary:
Senators Close To Deal To End Airport TSA Crisis As Americans' Fury Mounts

            With no-shows by unpaid TSA agents imposing hours-long security-line waits and missed flights on flyers at major airports across the country, senators on Monday night signaled that a deal to end the TSA crisis is finally within sight.

title: Dry Van Spot Rates Highest Since 2022 As Spring Tightens Capacity
summary:
Dry Van Spot Rates Highest Since 2022 As Spring Tightens Capacity

            Submitted by FreightWaves,

The freight market momentum is building at a rapid clip. 

National dry van spot rates — tracked via the SONAR National Truckload Index, the 7-day moving average of booked rates including fuel — have broken out to a new cycle high of $2.89 per mile.



This represents the strongest level since 2022 and confirms the market’s shift toward carriers is gaining real traction.

title: Wall Street's Trillion-Dollar Bet On "Tax Alpha"
summary:
Wall Street's Trillion-Dollar Bet On "Tax Alpha"

            Tax alpha — the practice of improving investment returns by reducing taxes — has become one of the fastest-growing strategies on Wall Street, according to Bloomberg. 

Rather than focusing only on beating the market, many investment firms now design portfolios to minimize taxes, often producing higher after-tax returns even if pre-tax performance is similar to traditional strategies.

title: How former Acorda CEO Ron Cohen landed at a Parkinson’s cell therapy startup
summary: Cohen says he looked at around two dozen companies since Acorda wound down. Oryon Cell Therapies, with its funding, data and “autologous” approach, stood out amongst the crowd.

title: Pfizer, Valneva to seek approval of Lyme disease vaccine despite mixed study results
summary: The partners said the shot produced “clinically meaningful” efficacy despite missing its main study objective, a finding they blamed on a lower-than-expected rate of infections during the trial.

title: Roche stops work on experimental SMA drug
summary: In a letter to European patients, Roche said its decision to end development of “emugrobart” was based on the drug “not consistently” improving muscle growth and motor function in a key study.

summary: Acquiring a unit of privately held Synnovation Therapeutics hands Novartis a prospect that might improve on medicines that, like its own Piqray, target tumors driven to growth by PIK3CA mutations.

summary: Imcivree’s approval in a type of obesity driven by brain injury could unlock an opportunity Wall Street analysts believe to be worth more than $1 billion.

title: Novo uses FDA voucher to win speedy approval of higher-dose Wegovy
summary: The clearance, issued 54 days after an approval submission, gives Novo another chance to claw back market share from Eli Lilly.

title: Ocugen eye disease gene therapy hits ph. 2 targets but underwhelms investors
summary: Full 12-month data from Ocugen’s phase 2 eye disease gene therapy show the therapy significantly reduces the size of lesions, though the treatment’s smaller effect compared to an earlier preliminary analysis sent the biotech’s stock down a tad.

title: Shionogi pays Apnimed $100M for joint venture to awaken sleep disorder dream
summary: Shionogi has struck a deal to take full control of its sleep disorder joint venture, agreeing to pay Apnimed $100 million upfront for its partner’s half of the business.

title: Merck hands over $20M in IBD drug target deal with Flagship's Quotient
summary: Looking to devise new drug targets for inflammatory bowel disease, Merck will pay Flagship Pioneering's Quotient Therapeutics $20 million upfront for access to the biotech’s somatic genomics platform.

title: Virtual Specialty Care Has Time. Will We Use It Wisely?
summary:
The specialty access and capacity crisis demands more than incremental fixes. It requires expanding virtual access and fundamentally rethinking how specialty care can be delivered.

title: AI Won’t Replace Clinical Trial Teams, But It Fixes the Bottlenecks That Keep Patients Out
summary:
We don’t have a shortage of willing patients — instead we have a shortage of scalable ways to identify and engage them responsibly. AI, used meaningfully offers a reliable path forward. 
The post AI Won’t Replace Clinical Trial Teams, But It Fixes the Bottlenecks That Keep Patients Out appeared first on MedCity News.

title: Why Pure DTC Doesn’t Work in Healthcare, Per Muse Capital
summary:
Rachel Springate, general partner at Muse Capital, said the next wave of healthcare startups will be driven by consumer demand — but pure direct-to-consumer models rarely work. Companies like Midi Health, Mavida Health and Mahmee are showing a different path: starting with patients, collecting outcomes data, and integrating with clinicians, payers and the broader healthcare system.
The post Why Pure DTC Doesn’t Work in Healthcare, Per Muse Capital appeared first on MedCity News.

title: Why Novartis Is Paying $2B for a Cancer Drug That Hits the Same Target as One of Its Own Products
summary:
Novartis already markets the PI3Kα inhibitor Piqray for breast cancer, but acquiring a Synnovation Therapeutics subsidiary brings an early clinical program that the pharma company claims could be best in the class of next-generation therapies for the target. Competitors include Eli Lilly, Relay Therapeutics, and OnKure Therapeutics. 
The post Why Novartis Is Paying $2B for a Cancer Drug That Hits the Same Target as One of Its Own Products appeared first on MedCity News.

title: Imaging Interoperability Offers a Lifeline to Rural Hospitals and the Patients Depending on Them
summary:
Typically, rural hospitals operate as “islands,” where a patient might receive an initial scan at a community health facility, see a specialist at a regional medical center, and return home for follow-up care. In this scenario, it’s common for their medical images to become trapped in three different systems.
The post Imaging Interoperability Offers a Lifeline to Rural Hospitals and the Patients Depending on Them appeared first on MedCity News.

title: Why GI Providers Should Care About That “Health Hack”
summary:
Patients want to explore more holistic options and are becoming more educated and interested in their own health. However, this trend of growing trust in unchecked online health advice often introduces more risk to patients than help.
The post Why GI Providers Should Care About That “Health Hack” appeared first on MedCity News.

title: The Saga of Utah’s Rx Refill Bot: A Bold Bet on AI & Researchers Who Cried Foul
summary:
Utah is piloting an AI system developed by Doctronic to autonomously handle routine prescription refills, aiming to reduce delays and boost medication adherence. But a report from Mindgard AI claiming that there are vulnerabilities in the company’s chatbot underscores the broader challenge regulators and developers face in ensuring healthcare AI systems remain safe and reliable in real-world use.
